title: debt_report.md summary reflects real per-gate step conclusions (fix spurious ERROR rows)
intent: Fix app.ci.generate_debt_report so its Summary table reflects the actual debt-gates step conclusions instead of rendering a spurious '| <check> | ERROR | - | - |' row for every check. Root cause: run_check ran each check subprocess with cwd=_repo_root()/services/api, but _repo_root() already resolves to services/api (it matches the nested services/api/pyproject.toml first), so the cwd became services/api/services/api (non-existent) and every subprocess raised -> ERROR. This misled a reviewer into reading a green debt-gates job as red. Fix cwd by walking up to the services/api package root, fail explicitly when required baseline files are missing, and run the ratchet checks (complexity, file size) plus duplication with the same arguments used by the debt-gates workflow. No workflow, product, or gate-threshold change.

diff_articulation.what_changed: (1) generate_debt_report.py: _api_dir() now walks upward from this module (or a supplied start path in tests) and selects the nearest directory containing app/ci plus pyproject.toml, replacing the broken _repo_root()/services/api (== services/api/services/api) cwd while avoiding a brittle fixed parents[2] dependency. (2) run_check can declare required_paths and returns a named ERROR when required debt-gates baseline files are absent, rather than failing later with an ambiguous subprocess error. Non-zero subprocesses that emit JSON still preserve that JSON verdict; non-zero subprocesses without JSON report stderr and exit_code. (3) The complexity and file-size summary rows now run in ratchet mode with their committed baselines, matching the debt-gates workflow step. Duplication now runs with the workflow's --threshold 100 argument and the Summary threshold cell also reports 100 groups instead of the stale 50 groups text. Detail headers renamed 'Worst Complexity'/'Large Files' -> 'New Complexity Violations'/'New Large Files' to match the ratchet data. Added a note under the summary explaining that status mirrors the debt-gates conclusions and ERROR means the check could not be run, not that the gate failed. (4) NEW tests/test_generate_debt_report.py: locks API-root discovery, missing-baseline reporting, subprocess JSON/non-JSON failure handling, parsed summary status assertions that do not depend on radon being installed, and duplication workflow-threshold alignment.
diff_articulation.why_not_redundant: The summary rendered ERROR for all five checks on both main and PRs even when the debt-gates job passed (every gate STEP concluded success). That directly misled a reviewer into nearly blocking a valid closure (PR #187). No existing test covered the report generator, so the cwd bug shipped silently. This makes the debt-gates report faithful to the gate it reports on and adds a regression lock.

commands_run: py -3.11 -m app.ci.generate_debt_report --output debt_report.md -> Summary shows Complexity/File size/Bare except/Safety fences/Duplication = PASS; no '| ERROR |' rows; Duplication threshold = 100 groups
python -m app.ci.check_complexity --baseline app/ci/complexity_baseline.json -> OK (no new violations)
python -m app.ci.check_file_sizes --baseline app/ci/file_sizes_baseline.json -> OK (no new violations)

files.intent: Fix run_check cwd via API-root discovery, require baseline files for ratchet checks, and mirror workflow arguments/status text including the duplication threshold.
files.behavior_change: report-output-only - debt_report.md summary now shows real per-check status; no gate threshold or workflow change

files.intent: Regression lock: API-root discovery, missing-baseline reporting, subprocess failure handling, parsed summary status, and duplication workflow-threshold alignment.
Per-PR CBSP21 manifest for this fix.
